steps.title: Identify the Goal and Key Signal
steps.content: The task requires placing the Declaration in context of Valle's changing beliefs. The decisive signal from the notes is the cause-and-effect sequence: Valle opposed independence, the 1820 revolt changed his mind, then he wrote the Declaration.

steps.title: Apply the Signal to Find the Match
steps.content:
B directly connects these elements: Valle as a “long an opponent,” the “1820 revolt” as what changed his position, and his writing the “1821 declaration” as the result.

The other three choices either misrepresent when the change occurred (C cites 1814, not 1820), use indirect phrasing that obscures the connection (A's “inspiration...needed”), or employ speculation rather than a clear causal link (D's “may not have happened”).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
